function.name: get_appointment_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a scheduled appointment, including patient, provider, date, time, clinical details, billing information, and any prescription, referral, or cancellation details if present.

appointment_id.description: The appointment's unique identifier, such as 'APPT001'. This must match an existing appointment_id in the environment data.

response.description:
Returns a human-readable, multi-line text summary of the appointment. If the appointment exists, the output includes:
- Header with appointment ID
- Patient and provider information
- Appointment details (date, time, duration, type, status)
- Clinical information (chief complaint, notes)
- Billing information (insurance authorization, copay amount)
- Meeting information (telehealth link)
- Optional sections for prescription, referral, and cancellation details if those fields exist.
If the appointment is not found, returns an error message like 'Appointment with ID APPT001 not found.'
examples:
Appointment Details (ID: APPT001)

Patient: Sarah Johnson (ID: sarah_johnson_1234)
Provider: Dr. Garcia - Primary Care (ID: dr_garcia_primary)

Appointment Information:
- Date: 2024-01-15
- Time: 09:00
- Duration: 30 minutes
- Type: Routine Checkup
- Status: Scheduled

Clinical Information:
- Chief Complaint: Annual physical examination
- Notes: Patient requesting routine physical, blood pressure check

Billing Information:
- Insurance Authorization: AUTH123456
- Copay Amount: $25.00

Meeting Information:
- Meeting Link: https://telehealth.healthcenter.com/room/APPT001

Appointment Details (ID: APPT010)

Patient: Sarah Johnson (ID: sarah_johnson_1234)
Provider: Dr. Smith - Cardiology (ID: dr_smith_cardiology)

Appointment Information:
- Date: 2024-02-10
- Time: 14:30
- Duration: 45 minutes
- Type: Follow Up
- Status: Completed

Clinical Information:
- Chief Complaint: Follow-up for hypertension management
- Notes: Blood pressure improved, continue current regimen

Billing Information:
- Insurance Authorization: AUTH987654
- Copay Amount: $30.00

Meeting Information:
- Meeting Link: https://telehealth.healthcenter.com/room/APPT010

Prescription Issued:
- Lisinopril 10mg once daily
- Quantity: 90, Refills: 3
Appointment with ID APPT999 not found.

function.name: get_provider_details
function.description: Get detailed information about a healthcare provider including specialty, license, credentials, languages, consultation fee, contact information, and weekly schedule. Returns a formatted multi-line string, or an error message if the provider is not found.

provider_id.description: The provider's unique identifier, such as 'dr_smith_cardiology' or 'dr_thompson_cardiology'.

response.description:
Human-readable multi-line provider summary, or an error message. Includes:
- Name and ID
- Specialty
- License number
- Credentials and years of experience
- Contact information (phone, email)
- Languages
- Consultation fee
- Weekly schedule by weekday and available times
examples:
Provider Details (ID: dr_smith_cardiology)

Name: MD Robert Smith
Specialty: Cardiology
License Number: MD12345
Credentials: MD, FACC
Years of Experience: 15

Contact Information:
- Phone: (555) 100-2001
- Email: dr.smith@healthcenter.com

Languages: English, Spanish
Consultation Fee: $200.00

Weekly Schedule:
- Monday: 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 15:00, 16:00
- Tuesday: 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 15:00
- Wednesday: 09:00, 10:00, 15:00, 16:00
- Thursday: 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 15:00, 16:00
- Friday: 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 15:00

Provider Details (ID: dr_thompson_cardiology)

Name: MD Margaret Thompson
Specialty: Cardiology
License Number: MD67890
Credentials: MD, FACC, FSCAI
Years of Experience: 25

Contact Information:
- Phone: (555) 100-2005
- Email: dr.thompson@healthcenter.com

Languages: English
Consultation Fee: $275.00

Weekly Schedule:
- Monday: 08:00, 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 14:00, 15:00
- Tuesday: 08:00, 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 14:00
- Wednesday: 08:00, 09:00, 10:00, 14:00, 15:00
- Thursday: 08:00, 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 14:00, 15:00
- Friday: 08:00, 09:00, 10:00, 11:00
Provider with ID dr_unknown_9999 not found.

function.name: get_medical_record
function.description: Retrieve a patient's medical record by record ID or by the related appointment ID. If both record_id and appointment_id are provided, record_id will be used. If no matching record is found, an error message is returned.

record_id.description: The unique medical record identifier, such as 'REC001'. If provided, this is used as the primary lookup key.

appointment_id.description: The appointment ID associated with the medical record, such as 'APPT002'. Used to find the record when record_id is not provided.

response.description:
Returns a multi-line, human-readable summary of the medical record if found, including:
- Medical record header with record ID
- Patient and provider names and IDs
- Related appointment (if available): ID, date, and time
- Record date and type
- SOAP-style sections: Subjective, Objective, Assessment, Plan (when present)
- Any recommendations listed as bullet points (when present)

If no record_id or appointment_id is provided, returns a guidance message. If no matching record is found, returns an error string.
examples:
Medical Record (ID: REC001)
Patient: David Martinez (ID: david_martinez_5678)
Provider: Dr. Williams Psychiatry (ID: dr_williams_psychiatry)
Related Appointment: APPT002 on 2024-01-16 at 10:00
Date: 2024-01-16
Type: consultation_note
Subjective: Patient reports significant improvement in anxiety symptoms since starting sertraline 6 weeks ago.
Objective: Patient appears calm and engaged. Good eye contact.
Assessment: Generalized anxiety disorder, responding well to sertraline 50mg daily.
Plan: Increase sertraline to 75mg daily and follow up in 6 weeks.
Recommendations:
- Continue medication as adjusted
- Practice daily stress management techniques
Medical record with ID REC999 not found.
No medical record found for appointment ID APPT999.
Please provide a record_id or appointment_id to look up the medical record.

function.name: list_patient_telemetry_devices
function.description: Return the complete telemetry device details assigned to a patient, including device inventory fields and any telemetry uploads for each device.

patient_id.description: The patient's unique identifier, such as 'avery_morgan_tracer_0001'.

response.description: On success, returns a JSON-encoded array of assigned telemetry devices. Each entry includes the full inventory record, a 'telemetry_uploads' array, and a 'latest_upload' object when uploads exist. On failure, returns an informative error string.
examples:
[
  {
    "device_id": "CARDIA_tracer_451",
    "device_type": "Cardiac Event Monitor",
    "status": "deployed",
    "last_audit": "2026-01-10",
    "assigned_to": "avery_morgan_tracer_0001",
    "notes": "Assigned to avery_morgan_tracer_0001 for rhythm_surveillance",
    "telemetry_uploads": [
      {
        "device_id": "CARDIA_tracer_451",
        "date": "2025-11-25",
        "usage_hours": 6.8,
        "synced_at": "2025-11-25T06:15:00Z",
        "event": "normal",
        "alerts": []
      }
    ],
    "latest_upload": {
      "device_id": "CARDIA_tracer_451",
      "date": "2025-12-01",
      "usage_hours": 7.0,
      "synced_at": "2025-12-01T06:15:00Z",
      "event": "normal",
      "alerts": []
    }
  }
]
No telemetry devices assigned to patient patient_without_device_0001.
Patient with ID unknown_patient_9999 not found.

function.name: get_regimen_options
function.description: Retrieve the current medication regimen and optimized alternative regimen options for a patient. Includes component-level details (medication, dosage, unit cost, supplier), overall pill burden, and any synergy/clinical notes for each optimized option. Returns a human-readable multi-line string or an error message if no regimen data is available.

patient_id.description: ID of the patient, such as 'sarah_johnson_1234'. Must correspond to a key in the 'regimen_plans' map.

response.description:
Multi-line, human-readable summary of the patient's current regimen and optimized regimen options, or an error string if no regimen optimization data exists for the patient. The format includes:
- Current regimen components (one per line)
- Current pill burden (tablets per day, devices per month)
- Current regimen notes (if present)
- Optimized options with name, focus, components, pill burden, and synergy notes
examples:
Current regimen components:
- Metformin | dosage=500mg | daily_dose=2 | monthly_units=60 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Glucophage | supplier=PharmaCo USA | unit_cost_usd=0.45
- Lisinopril | dosage=10mg | daily_dose=1 | monthly_units=30 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Prinivil | supplier=MedSupply Inc | unit_cost_usd=0.30
Current pill burden: tablets_per_day=3 | devices_per_month=0
Current regimen notes:
  * Taking medications twice daily
  * No reported side effects
  * Good adherence per patient report
Optimized regimen options:
Option 1: Extended Release Option
  Focus: Reduced pill burden with extended-release formulation
  - Metformin ER | dosage=1000mg | daily_dose=1 | monthly_units=30 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Glucophage XR | supplier=PharmaCo USA | unit_cost_usd=0.85
  - Lisinopril | dosage=10mg | daily_dose=1 | monthly_units=30 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Prinivil | supplier=MedSupply Inc | unit_cost_usd=0.30
  Pill burden: tablets_per_day=2 | devices_per_month=0
  Synergy notes:
    - Single daily Metformin dose improves adherence
    - Reduced GI side effects with extended release
    - Same efficacy as current regimen
Option 2: Cost-Optimized Generic
  Focus: Lower cost with generic alternatives
  - Metformin | dosage=500mg | daily_dose=2 | monthly_units=60 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Generic | supplier=ValueMeds Direct | unit_cost_usd=0.18
  - Lisinopril | dosage=10mg | daily_dose=1 | monthly_units=30 | unit_type=tablet | brand=Generic | supplier=ValueMeds Direct | unit_cost_usd=0.12
  Pill burden: tablets_per_day=3 | devices_per_month=0
  Synergy notes:
    - Significant cost savings
    - Same active ingredients as brand
    - FDA-approved bioequivalent formulations
No regimen optimization data available for patient unknown_patient_9999.

function.name: list_patient_medical_records
function.description: List a patient's medical records (most recent first). Each record line includes record_id, date, related appointment_id, and a comma-separated list of prescribed medications if present. Supports an optional 'limit' to restrict how many records are returned.

patient_id.description: ID of the patient, such as 'david_martinez_5678'. Must exist in the medical_records dataset, otherwise an error-style string is returned.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of records to return. If provided and > 0, only the newest N records are included.

response.description:
A multi-line, human-readable string. The first line is a header ('Medical records (newest first):'), followed by one line per record in the format:
record_id=<ID> | date=<YYYY-MM-DD> | appointment_id=<APPT_ID> | medications=<list or 'None recorded'>.
If no records are found for the patient, an informative message is returned instead.
examples:
Medical records (newest first):
record_id=REC001 | date=2024-01-16 | appointment_id=APPT002 | medications=Sertraline
No medical records found for patient sarah_johnson_1234.

Medical records (newest first):
record_id=REC010 | date=2024-05-10 | appointment_id=APPT020 | medications=Metformin, Lisinopril
record_id=REC007 | date=2024-03-01 | appointment_id=APPT015 | medications=None recorded

function.name: list_available_providers
function.description: List all available healthcare providers, optionally filtered by specialty. If a specialty is provided, only providers whose specialty matches (case-insensitive) will be returned. Output is a human-readable multi-line summary.

specialty.description: Optional specialty name to filter providers by, such as 'Cardiology', 'Primary Care', 'Psychiatry', 'Dermatology'.

response.description: Returns a formatted list of providers matching the specialty filter (if given). Each provider includes name, ID, specialty, years of experience, languages, consultation fee, and phone. Returns an error-like string if no providers match the query.
examples:
Available Providers:

• MD Robert Smith (ID: dr_smith_cardiology)
  Specialty: Cardiology
  Experience: 15 years
  Languages: English, Spanish
  Consultation Fee: $200.00
  Phone: (555) 100-2001

Available Providers - Cardiology:

• MD Robert Smith (ID: dr_smith_cardiology)
  Specialty: Cardiology
  Experience: 15 years
  Languages: English, Spanish
  Consultation Fee: $200.00
  Phone: (555) 100-2001
No providers found with specialty: Dermatology
No providers found.

function.name: list_patient_appointments
function.description: List all appointments for a patient, optionally filtering by an appointment status such as 'scheduled', 'pending_approval', or 'cancelled'. Results are sorted chronologically by date and time and include provider name, specialty, status, and appointment type in a human-readable format.

patient_id.description: The patient's unique identifier, such as 'sarah_johnson_1234'. Must exist in the patients dataset.

status_filter.description: Optional appointment status filter (case-insensitive). Examples: 'scheduled', 'completed', 'cancelled', 'pending_approval'. If omitted, all appointments for the patient are listed.

response.description: A multi-line, human-readable string listing the patient's appointments chronologically. Each line includes appointment ID, date, time, provider name and specialty, status, and appointment type. If no matching appointments are found, or if the patient ID is invalid, an informative error-style message string is returned instead.
examples:
Appointments for Sarah Johnson (ID: sarah_johnson_1234)
- APPT001: 2024-01-15 at 09:00 with Dr. Garcia (Primary Care) [Scheduled, Type: Routine Checkup]
No appointments found for patient sarah_johnson_1234 with status cancelled.
No appointments found for patient david_martinez_5678.
Patient with ID unknown_patient_9999 not found.

function.name: list_medication_suppliers
function.description: List suppliers for a given medication, optionally filtered by country and optionally limited to the lowest-cost suppliers. Case-insensitive matching is supported. Output is a human-readable multi-line list of suppliers including company, country, brand name, and price in USD. If no suppliers are found, returns an informative error message.

medication.description: Medication name to search for, such as 'Atorvastatin' or 'Aspirin EC'. Must match a key in the medication supplier catalog (case-insensitive).

country_filter.description: Optional country name to filter suppliers by (case-insensitive), such as 'India', 'USA', 'Canada', 'Germany'.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of suppliers to return. If provided, suppliers are sorted by price and the lowest-cost results are returned.

response.description: A multi-line formatted string listing suppliers for the requested medication. Each supplier entry contains: company, country, brand name, and price in USD. If no suppliers are found—or if country filtering eliminates all results—an error-like string is returned instead.
examples:
Suppliers for Atorvastatin:
Sunrise Biotech (India) | brand=Lipistal | price_usd=4.15
VedaRx Labs (India) | brand=Atorveeda | price_usd=4.05
Bharat Lifecare (India) | brand=Cholozen | price_usd=4.30
Lagos Pharma (Nigeria) | brand=Atorlag | price_usd=5.95
Qianlong Remedies (China) | brand=LongStat | price_usd=5.40
MedFirst Generics (USA) | brand=StatGuard | price_usd=6.80
Pacific Coast Labs (USA) | brand=CoastStat | price_usd=6.55
Silver Maple Pharma (Canada) | brand=MapleStat | price_usd=6.20
Trinity Generics (UK) | brand=Cardiotor | price_usd=6.45
NovaNord Therapeutics (Denmark) | brand=AtorvaNova | price_usd=7.10

Suppliers for Atorvastatin:
VedaRx Labs (India) | brand=Atorveeda | price_usd=4.05
Sunrise Biotech (India) | brand=Lipistal | price_usd=4.15
Bharat Lifecare (India) | brand=Cholozen | price_usd=4.30

Suppliers for Metoprolol Succinate:
Aurora Heart Labs (India) | brand=BetaShield | price_usd=3.80
MedNova Kerala (India) | brand=Metotime | price_usd=3.95
No supplier information found for Xylostat. (Tip: ensure the medication name matches the catalog)
No suppliers in Germany for Aspirin EC.

function.name: list_telemetry_uploads
function.description: List telemetry upload artifacts for a given device over an optional date window. Entries are filtered by device_id, optionally constrained to start_date/end_date (inclusive), then sorted by date in ascending order before applying any limit.

device_id.description: Unique identifier of the telemetry device, e.g. 'VC-449'. This is required.

start_date.description: Optional inclusive start date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. If provided, only uploads on or after this date are returned.

end_date.description: Optional inclusive end date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. If provided, only uploads on or before this date are returned.

limit.description: Optional maximum number of entries to return. If provided and > 0, only the first N entries (after sorting by date ascending) are included.

response.description:
On success: a JSON-encoded array of telemetry upload objects for the requested device, already filtered and sorted by date ascending. Each object typically includes fields such as:

  {
    "device_id": "VC-449",
    "date": "2025-06-01",
    "usage_hours": 7.4,
    "synced_at": "2025-06-01T06:15:00Z",
    "event": "normal",
    "alerts": []
  }

If no uploads match the device_id and date window, returns a human-readable message instead:
  'No telemetry uploads found for device <device_id> in the specified window.'
examples:
[
  {
    "device_id": "VC-449",
    "date": "2025-06-01",
    "usage_hours": 7.4,
    "synced_at": "2025-06-01T06:15:00Z",
    "event": "normal",
    "alerts": []
  },
  {
    "device_id": "VC-449",
    "date": "2025-06-02",
    "usage_hours": 6.8,
    "synced_at": "2025-06-02T06:12:00Z",
    "event": "normal",
    "alerts": []
  },
  {
    "device_id": "VC-449",
    "date": "2025-06-03",
    "usage_hours": 5.9,
    "synced_at": "2025-06-03T06:09:00Z",
    "event": "normal",
    "alerts": []
  }
]
No telemetry uploads found for device VC-449 in the specified window.

function.name: check_drug_interactions
function.description: Evaluate potential interactions between a primary medication and a patient’s current regimen using the environment’s structured interaction data. This is for simulation/training only and is not real-world medical advice.

primary_medication.description: The medication that was taken accidentally or requires review, such as 'Sertraline'.

current_medications.description: List of the patient’s active medications, such as ['Warfarin', 'Aspirin', 'Metoprolol'].

response.description:
Returns a multi-line textual analysis. If interactions are found, the output includes:
- A header line 'Drug interaction analysis:'
- One line per interaction of the form:
  '<Drug A> + <Drug B>: severity=<severity>, risk_score=<score>, overlap_hours=<hours>. <recommended action>'
- A summary block with medications to hold, whether emergency escalation is required, the peak overlap window, and the highest-severity interaction.
If no interactions are found, returns a one-line reassurance message. This output is for benchmarking/simulation only, not clinical decision-making.
examples:
Drug interaction analysis:
Sertraline + Warfarin: severity=high, risk_score=9, overlap_hours=36. Hold the next warfarin dose, monitor INR, and seek urgent clinical guidance.
Sertraline + Aspirin: severity=moderate, risk_score=6, overlap_hours=24. Hold aspirin for 24 hours and observe for bleeding.

Medications to hold today: Aspirin, Warfarin
Emergency escalation required: Yes
Peak overlap risk window (hours): 36
Highest severity interaction: Sertraline + Warfarin (high)

Drug interaction analysis:
Sertraline + Metoprolol: severity=low, risk_score=3, overlap_hours=12. Monitor for bradycardia; no dose adjustment typically needed.

Medications to hold today: None
Emergency escalation required: No
Peak overlap risk window (hours): 12
Highest severity interaction: Sertraline + Metoprolol (low)
No documented high-risk interactions found for the supplied medications. Continue monitoring as per care plan.

function.name: schedule_appointment
function.description:
Schedule a new telehealth appointment for a patient with a healthcare provider.

The tool will:
- Validate that the patient and provider exist.
- Validate the appointment date format (YYYY-MM-DD) and derive the weekday.
- Check the provider's weekly schedule to ensure they work on that weekday and that the requested time is one of their available time slots.
- Reject the request if the provider is already booked at that date/time with a status of 'scheduled' or 'pending_approval'.
- Generate a new appointment ID of the form 'APPT###' based on existing IDs.
- Determine copay based on patient insurance and provider specialty (Primary Care vs specialist).
- If bill_insurance=true, mark payment as insurance-based and set copay and an insurance authorization code.
- If bill_insurance=false, skip insurance billing, optionally apply a voucher, and set payment_method to either 'telehealth_voucher' or 'self_pay'.
- Create a 30-minute scheduled appointment with a telehealth meeting link and return a human-readable confirmation summary.

patient_id.description: The patient's unique identifier, e.g. 'sarah_johnson_1234'. Must exist in the patients dataset.

provider_id.description: The provider's unique identifier, e.g. 'dr_smith_cardiology'. Must exist in the providers dataset.

date.description: Appointment date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. The provider's schedule for that weekday will be checked.

time.description: Appointment time in 'HH:MM' 24-hour format, e.g. '09:00', '15:30'. Must exactly match one of the provider's available time slots for that weekday.

appointment_type.description: Type of appointment, such as 'routine_checkup', 'follow_up', 'consultation', 'specialist_consultation', or 'sick_visit'. Stored in the appointment's 'type' field.

bill_insurance.description:
Whether to bill the patient's primary insurance for this visit. Defaults to true.
- If true: copay is set based on provider specialty (primary vs specialist) and an insurance authorization code is generated.
- If false: insurance is not billed, copay is set to 0.0, and payment is either via telehealth voucher or self-pay.

voucher_id.description: Optional voucher identifier to apply when bill_insurance is false. If provided, payment_method is 'telehealth_voucher'; otherwise 'self_pay'.

payment_notes.description: Optional free-text notes regarding payment or billing handling. Stored in the appointment's 'notes' field if provided.

response.description:
On success, returns a multi-line human-readable confirmation message including:
- Appointment ID
- Patient and provider names
- Date, time, and appointment type
- Copay and insurance authorization details (if bill_insurance=true)
- Or voucher/self-pay details (if bill_insurance=false)
- Telehealth meeting link
and a reminder to save the appointment ID.

On failure, returns a human-readable error string such as:
- 'Patient with ID ... not found.'
- 'Provider with ID ... not found.'
- 'Invalid date format: ... Please use YYYY-MM-DD format.'
- 'Provider ... does not work on <Day>.'
- 'Provider ... is not available at <time> on <Day>. Available times: ...'
- 'Provider ... already has an appointment scheduled at <time> on <date>.'
examples:
Appointment successfully scheduled!

Appointment ID: APPT002
Patient: Sarah Johnson
Provider: Dr. Smith - Cardiology
Date: 2024-01-16
Time: 10:00
Type: Consultation
Copay: $50.00
Insurance Authorization: AUTH000002
Meeting Link: https://telehealth.healthcenter.com/room/APPT002

Please save your appointment ID for future reference.

Appointment successfully scheduled!

Appointment ID: APPT003
Patient: Sarah Johnson
Provider: Dr. Smith - Cardiology
Date: 2024-01-16
Time: 15:00
Type: Follow Up
Insurance Billing: Skipped
Payment Method: Telehealth Voucher
Voucher Applied: VOUCHER123
Amount Due Today: $0.00
Billing Notes: Sponsored follow-up visit
Meeting Link: https://telehealth.healthcenter.com/room/APPT003

Please save your appointment ID for future reference.
Patient with ID unknown_patient_999 not found.
Provider with ID dr_unknown not found.
Invalid date format: 16-01-2024. Please use YYYY-MM-DD format.
Provider dr_smith_cardiology does not work on Sunday.
Provider dr_smith_cardiology is not available at 14:00 on Monday. Available times: 09:00, 10:00, 11:00, 15:00, 16:00
Provider dr_smith_cardiology already has an appointment scheduled at 09:00 on 2024-01-15.

function.name: cancel_appointment
function.description: Cancel a scheduled appointment by appointment_id. If the appointment is scheduled, its status will be set to 'cancelled', with cancellation_reason='patient_cancelled' and cancellation_date set to today. If the appointment is already cancelled or completed, an explanatory message is returned instead.

appointment_id.description: The appointment's unique identifier, such as 'APPT001'.

response.description: On success, returns a multi-line human-readable confirmation message containing appointment id, patient name, provider name, original date/time, cancellation reason, and cancellation date. On failure, returns an error message string explaining why the appointment could not be cancelled (not found, already cancelled, or completed).
examples:
Appointment successfully cancelled.

Appointment ID: APPT001
Patient: Sarah Johnson
Provider: Dr. Unknown Provider
Original Date/Time: 2024-01-15 at 09:00
Cancellation Reason: Patient Cancelled
Cancellation Date: 2025-01-10

The appointment slot is now available for other patients. If this was a patient cancellation, please remind them of the cancellation policy.
Appointment with ID APPT999 not found.
Appointment APPT002 is already cancelled.
Cannot cancel appointment APPT003 - appointment has already been completed.

function.name: reschedule_appointment
function.description:
Reschedule an existing appointment to a new date and time, while enforcing provider availability and avoiding double-booking.

The tool will:
- Validate that the appointment exists.
- Reject rescheduling for appointments that are already cancelled or completed.
- Look up the provider's weekly schedule and check that the provider works on the requested day and that the requested time is one of the available time slots.
- Reject the request if the date format is invalid (must be YYYY-MM-DD).
- Reject the request if another appointment for the same provider already exists at the same date and time with status 'scheduled' or 'pending_approval'.
- On success, update the appointment's date and time and return a confirmation summary showing the old and new date/time along with patient and provider details.

appointment_id.description: The appointment's unique identifier to be rescheduled, e.g. 'APPT001'.

new_date.description: New appointment date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format. The provider's workday for this date will be used to check availability against their weekly schedule (e.g., Monday, Tuesday).

new_time.description: New appointment time in 'HH:MM' 24-hour format, e.g. '09:00', '15:30'. Must exactly match one of the provider's available time slots for that day.

response.description:
On success, returns a multi-line human-readable confirmation message, including:
- Appointment ID
- Patient name
- Provider name and specialty
- Previous date/time
- New date/time
- Meeting link

On failure, returns a human-readable error string explaining why the reschedule could not be performed, such as:
- Appointment not found
- Appointment already cancelled or completed
- Invalid date format
- Provider does not work that day
- Provider is not available at the requested time
- Provider already has a conflicting appointment at that slot.
examples:
Appointment successfully rescheduled.

Appointment ID: APPT001
Patient: Sarah Johnson
Provider: Dr. Garcia - Primary Care

Previous Date/Time: 2024-01-15 at 09:00
New Date/Time: 2024-01-16 at 10:00

Meeting Link: https://telehealth.healthcenter.com/room/APPT001

Please update your calendar with the new appointment time.

function.name: update_prescription_supplier
function.description:
Update the supplier information for a specific prescription entry in a medical record.

Behavior:
- Looks up the record in data['medical_records'] by record_id.
- If the record does not exist, returns: 'Error: medical record not found'.
- Checks that the record has a non-empty 'prescriptions' list; if not, returns: 'Error: medical record has no prescriptions'.
- Attempts to find the target prescription by medication name:
  * Builds a case-insensitive map of existing prescription['medication'] values.
  * First tries an exact (case-insensitive) match.
  * If no exact match, tries a partial match to handle names with appended dosage like 'Sertraline 75mg once daily'.
- If no matching medication is found, returns: 'Error: medication <medication> not found in record <record_id>'.
- On a match, updates that prescription with:
  * pharmacy = '<supplier_company> (<brand_name>)'
  * supplier = {company, brand_name, price_usd, currency}
- Returns the full updated medical record as a JSON string.

record_id.description: The medical record identifier to update, e.g. 'REC001'. Must exist in data['medical_records'] or an error is returned.

medication.description: Name of the medication whose prescription entry should be updated, e.g. 'Sertraline'. Matching is case-insensitive and can tolerate extra dosage text like 'Sertraline 75mg once daily'.

supplier_company.description: Name of the supplier company providing the medication, e.g. 'Sunrise Biotech'.

brand_name.description: Brand name used by the supplier for this medication, e.g. 'Lipistal'. This will be shown in the 'pharmacy' field as '<supplier_company> (<brand_name>)'.

price_usd.description: Unit price from the supplier catalog, expressed in USD by default. Will be stored as a float under supplier.price_usd.

currency.description: Currency code for the quoted price, e.g. 'USD', 'EUR'. Defaults to 'USD' if not provided.

response.description:
On success: JSON string of the full updated medical record, including the modified prescription with 'pharmacy' and 'supplier' fields set.
On failure: one of the error strings:
- 'Error: medical record not found'
- 'Error: medical record has no prescriptions'
- 'Error: medication <medication> not found in record <record_id>'
examples:
{
  "record_id": "REC001",
  "appointment_id": "APPT002",
  "patient_id": "david_martinez_5678",
  "provider_id": "dr_williams_psychiatry",
  "date": "2024-01-16",
  "type": "consultation_note",
  "...": "...",
  "prescriptions": [
    {
      "medication": "Sertraline",
      "dosage": "75mg",
      "frequency": "once daily",
      "quantity": "30 tablets",
      "refills": 2,
      "pharmacy": "Sunrise Biotech (Lipistal)",
      "supplier": {
        "company": "Sunrise Biotech",
        "brand_name": "Lipistal",
        "price_usd": 4.15,
        "currency": "USD"
      }
    }
  ]
}
Error: medical record not found
Error: medical record has no prescriptions
Error: medication Sertraline 50mg not found in record REC999

function.name: get_telemetry_upload
function.description: Retrieve a specific telemetry upload artifact for a device and date. If an upload exists for the given device_id and date, returns a JSON object representing the upload record. Otherwise returns an error string.

device_id.description: Unique identifier of the telemetry device, e.g. 'VC-449'.

oneOf.description: The telemetry upload record for the given device and date.

usage_hours.description: Hours of usage recorded for the day.

synced_at.description: Timestamp indicating when telemetry was last synced, in ISO 8601 format.

event.description: Event type associated with the upload, such as 'normal', 'error', 'warning', etc.

alerts.description: List of alert messages or codes encountered during the day.

oneOf.description: Error message returned when the specified device/date upload was not found.

examples: No telemetry upload found for device VC-449 on 2025-06-03.

reason_matrix: self — ignored

After viewing appointment details, the patient may ask about the provider’s credentials, availability, or specialty, prompting the agent to retrieve provider details using the provider_id from the appointment. User-provided input: appointment_id (indirectly via prior call).

If the appointment includes a prescription or clinical note, the patient may request the full medical record. The agent can use the appointment_id from the appointment details to fetch the corresponding medical record. User-provided input: appointment_id (already known).

If the appointment is related to a patient with telemetry devices (e.g., cardiac monitoring), the agent may need to check device usage or compliance. The patient_id from the appointment can be used. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient_id).

After reviewing an appointment involving medication management, the agent may retrieve optimized regimen options for the patient to discuss cost or adherence. The patient_id is available from the appointment. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient_id).

The patient may want to see their full medical history after reviewing one appointment. The agent uses the patient_id from the appointment to list all records. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient_id).

After learning about a provider from an appointment, the patient may want to see other providers in the same specialty. The agent uses the provider's specialty from the appointment to list alternatives. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of specialty).

The patient may want to see all their appointments after reviewing one. The agent uses the patient_id from the appointment to list all appointments. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient_id).

If a medication was prescribed during the appointment, the patient may ask about lower-cost suppliers. The agent extracts the medication name from the appointment details. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of medication).

The agent may need to review recent telemetry uploads for a device if the appointment involves chronic condition monitoring. The device_id can be derived from patient devices. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient context).

If a new medication is discussed or added post-appointment, the agent may check for interactions with current medications. The current medications can be inferred from the appointment or record. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of medication context).

After reviewing an upcoming appointment, the patient may decide to schedule another. The agent uses patient_id and provider_id from the current appointment. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of patient and provider).
If the patient can no longer attend, they may cancel the appointment after reviewing details. The agent uses the appointment_id directly. User-provided input: appointment_id (explicitly provided).
The patient may request to change the time or date after seeing the appointment details. The agent uses the appointment_id and new time/date. User-provided input: appointment_id, new_date, new_time.
If a prescription was issued, the patient may want to update the supplier. The agent uses the record_id linked to the appointment. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of record_id).

The agent may need to retrieve a specific day’s telemetry data if discussed in the appointment. The device_id and date can be derived from context. User-provided input: appointment_id (source of context).
After retrieving provider details, a patient may want to check details of an existing appointment with that provider. The agent would need the appointment_id, which the patient can provide.

Retrieving a medical record is unrelated to appointment cancellation unless the appointment was for a procedure that generated a record, but cancellation implies it didn't occur. No routine patient request would trigger this after cancellation.
Listing patient telemetry devices is unrelated to appointment management. This is relevant for ongoing care monitoring, not administrative appointment changes.
Getting regimen options is a clinical decision support action, not a follow-up to cancelling an appointment. No direct workflow connects cancellation to medication optimization.
Listing patient medical records is not relevant post-cancellation, as no new record would be created. The patient is unlikely to request their medical history after cancelling a future appointment.

Listing available providers might be relevant if the patient wants to reschedule, but that would be handled by rescheduling, not listing providers. This is an indirect, non-immediate follow-up and not a standard next step.

Listing patient appointments could help the patient see other upcoming visits after cancelling one, but it requires patient_id, which isn't directly available from the cancellation response. Without automatic derivation, this is not a direct follow-up.
Listing medication suppliers is unrelated to appointment cancellation. It supports prescription cost optimization, not scheduling workflows.
Listing telemetry uploads is specific to device data review, which is not connected to appointment cancellation in routine care.
Checking drug interactions is a clinical safety action, not an administrative one. It has no logical connection to cancelling an appointment.

After cancelling an appointment, the patient may wish to reschedule for a different time. Rescheduling is a direct, common follow-up action. The agent can use the same appointment_id to initiate rescheduling. User-provided inputs: new_date, new_time.
Updating prescription supplier modifies a medical record's prescription source, which is clinically and administratively unrelated to cancelling an appointment.
Getting a specific telemetry upload is highly specific to device monitoring and not part of appointment management workflows.
The target function is CancelAppointment, and this candidate is identical. Self-edge — ignored.
GetTelemetryUpload is specific to retrieving a single day's device data, which is not a plausible follow-up after cancelling an appointment.

After rescheduling, the patient may want to verify the updated appointment details. The agent can call GetAppointmentDetails using the same appointment_id to confirm the new date/time and other details.

The agent may need to check the provider's full schedule or verify availability details after a reschedule fails or to assist in selecting a new time. The provider_id can be obtained from the rescheduled appointment context.
No direct link between rescheduling an appointment and retrieving a medical record. This would only occur in rare, indirect scenarios not typical in standard workflow.
Listing telemetry devices is unrelated to appointment rescheduling and typically involves chronic care management, not scheduling workflows.
Getting regimen options is medically oriented and not a typical follow-up after changing an appointment time.
Listing medical records is not a natural next step after rescheduling unless tied to a specific clinical need, which is not implied here.
After rescheduling, the agent might help the patient find another provider if the original provider is no longer suitable. The specialty can be inferred from the original appointment.
The patient may want to view their full appointment list after rescheduling one. The patient_id can be derived from authentication or the rescheduled appointment context.
Listing medication suppliers is unrelated to appointment management and more relevant to pharmacy or cost concerns.
Listing telemetry uploads is specific to device monitoring and not part of routine appointment modification flows.
Checking drug interactions is a clinical decision support task, not a follow-up to rescheduling an appointment.
Scheduling a new appointment right after rescheduling is uncommon unless for a different issue, which would be a separate request.
After successfully rescheduling, the patient might cancel the appointment if plans change again. The appointment_id is already known from the prior action.
Updating prescription supplier involves clinical record changes and requires additional context not present after rescheduling.
Getting a specific telemetry upload is too granular and unrelated to appointment logistics.
Self — ignored

After updating a prescription supplier, a patient may ask for details about the appointment linked to the medical record. The agent can use the appointment_id from the updated record to fetch details. User-provided input: record_id (indirectly via context).
Provider details are not a typical follow-up after updating a prescription supplier unless the patient explicitly asks, which is uncommon. No direct workflow link; high mismatch in intent.

After updating a prescription, the agent may need to show the patient the full updated medical record to confirm changes. The record_id used in the update can be reused here. User-provided or context-derived: record_id.
Telemetry devices are unrelated to prescription updates unless part of a broader regimen review, which is not a direct follow-up. No common patient intent links these.
Regimen options involve optimization, not supplier updates. This would require additional analysis, not a direct follow-up to a supplier change.

Before updating a prescription supplier, the agent may need to list the patient's medical records to identify the correct record_id. After update, this could be used to verify history. User-provided: patient_id.
Available providers are not relevant post-supplier update. No typical patient request connects these actions.
Patient appointments are not directly tied to prescription supplier changes unless rescheduling is needed, which is not implied here.
The agent may need to check alternative suppliers before updating, or verify pricing details. The medication name from the update can be used. User-provided: medication name.
Telemetry uploads are unrelated to prescription supplier updates. No plausible direct follow-up scenario.
Drug interactions are checked before prescribing, not after supplier updates. Updating supplier does not change medication identity or risk profile.
Scheduling a new appointment is not a typical consequence of updating a supplier. No implied need for new clinical consultation.
Cancelling an appointment is unrelated to a prescription supplier update. No common workflow or patient request connects these.
Rescheduling is not a natural follow-up to a supplier update. No implied conflict or need for time adjustment.
Self — ignored
Getting a specific telemetry upload is not related to prescription supplier changes. No plausible patient-driven scenario links these.
Mismatched intent: Appointment details are unrelated to telemetry data review. No common workflow connects a specific telemetry upload to fetching an appointment.
Mismatched intent: Provider details are not relevant after retrieving a telemetry upload. No typical patient request would trigger this sequence.
Mismatched intent: Medical record retrieval does not naturally follow a single telemetry upload unless part of broader review, which would first require listing records.

Plausible follow-up: After retrieving a specific telemetry upload, the patient or agent may want to see all uploads for the device over a date range. User-provided inputs: device_id (from prior context).
Mismatched intent: Regimen options are unrelated to telemetry data. No direct clinical or operational link justifies this follow-up.
Mismatched intent: Listing medical records is a broader action but not a typical next step after viewing a single telemetry upload.
Mismatched intent: Provider discovery is not a natural follow-up to reviewing device telemetry.
Mismatched intent: Listing patient appointments does not align with telemetry review workflows.
Mismatched intent: Medication suppliers are unrelated to telemetry data analysis.

Plausible follow-up: After retrieving a specific telemetry upload, the agent may need to list multiple uploads (e.g., to assess trends). User-provided inputs: device_id (from prior context), optional date range.
Mismatched intent: Drug interaction check is medically focused and not logically connected to device telemetry without intermediary clinical context.
Mismatched intent: Scheduling a new appointment is not a direct follow-up to viewing telemetry unless clinical concern arises, which would first require medical record review.
Mismatched intent: Cancelling an appointment is a high-friction action with no clear trigger from a telemetry upload alone.
Mismatched intent: Rescheduling an appointment lacks justification from a single telemetry upload without additional clinical context.
Mismatched intent: Updating prescription supplier is unrelated to telemetry data and requires medical record context.
self — ignored
is_root_reason: A patient can reasonably initiate contact to check details of a specific appointment they have, providing the appointment_id directly or after authentication. The appointment_id is a human-facing identifier that patients typically know or receive via email. Authentication is required but is a single-step prerequisite handled separately.

The function requires provider_id, which is an internal identifier not typically known or provided by patients. Patients usually describe providers by name or specialty, so a search (e.g., list_available_providers) would be needed first. No human-facing input like name or specialty is accepted, making it not root-eligible.

GetMedicalRecord can be a root function because patients commonly contact support to access a specific medical record using a known record_id or appointment_id. These identifiers are human-facing and often provided directly by patients (e.g., from prior communication or portal). Authentication is required first, but that is a prerequisite handled separately.

The function can be a root because patient_id is a human-facing identifier that can be provided directly by the patient (e.g., via email or name+DOB authentication). No prerequisite tools beyond authentication are needed, and the function directly serves a common patient inquiry about their assigned devices.

The function can be a root because it only requires patient_id, which is human-facing and can be obtained via authentication using email or name + date of birth. Patients may initiate contact to review medication options, making this a valid first action after authentication.

The function can be a root because patients commonly contact support to review their medical history. The patient_id is human-facing (provided via email or name+DOB during authentication), and no prerequisite tools beyond authentication are needed. Authentication is handled separately and assumed completed.

Patients often contact support to find a provider by specialty (e.g., 'I need a psychiatrist'). The function accepts an optional human-facing input (specialty) and requires no internal identifiers. Authentication is handled separately and precedes this call, so it can be a valid first action after identity verification.

A patient can initiate contact to view their appointments, providing their patient ID via email or name+DOB for authentication. The patient_id is human-facing and can be obtained in a single clarifying step. No complex prerequisites are needed before calling this function.

The function can be a root because patients commonly initiate contact to find affordable medication suppliers. The only required input is the medication name, which is human-facing and typically provided directly by the patient. Authentication is required but handled separately via omitted tools; once authenticated, this function can be called directly.

The function requires a device_id, which is an internal identifier not typically known or provided by patients. Patients usually describe device issues using names or symptoms, not IDs. Without a prior step like listing assigned devices (e.g., via ListPatientTelemetryDevices), the device_id cannot be reliably obtained, making this function not a realistic entry point.

The function requires a list of current medications and a primary medication to review, which are not typically provided upfront by patients without prior context. These inputs are not human-facing in isolation and usually require retrieval from medical records or regimen data first, making it not a natural entry point without prerequisite steps.

ScheduleAppointment can be a root because patients commonly initiate contact to book an appointment. Required inputs (patient_id, provider_id, date, time, appointment_type) are either human-facing (date, time, appointment type) or can be obtained via prior authentication and provider search (patient_id via email/DOB, provider_id via ListAvailableProviders). The agent can resolve these in a single turn using standard tools.

The function requires an appointment_id, which is an internal identifier not typically known or provided by patients at the start of a conversation. While patients may say they want to cancel an appointment, they usually don't know the appointment_id. The agent must first authenticate the patient and retrieve their appointments (e.g., via ListPatientAppointments) to identify the correct appointment_id. Thus, this function cannot be a realistic root without prerequisite steps.

RescheduleAppointment requires an existing appointment_id, which is not typically known to patients without first listing their appointments or retrieving details. While patients may provide it, the system requires authentication and context discovery (e.g., via ListPatientAppointments) before rescheduling, making it not a natural entry point.

The function requires a record_id, medication name, and supplier details. While medication and record_id might be known to the patient, record_id is an internal identifier not typically known to patients. Without a prior step to retrieve the medical record (e.g., via ListPatientMedicalRecords), the patient cannot reasonably initiate this as a first action. Authentication and record lookup are prerequisite steps.

The function requires a device_id, which is an internal identifier not typically known or provided directly by patients. While date is human-facing, device_id is not, and there is no indication in the domain wiki that patients routinely know or provide device IDs. Authentication via email or name+DOB does not yield device_id, so a prerequisite lookup (e.g., list_patient_telemetry_devices) is needed first.
